records.ety: [F. dominer, L. dominari: cf. OD. domineren to feast luxuriously. See Dominate, v. t.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Domineered (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Domineering.]

defs: To rule with insolence or arbitrary sway; to play the master; to be overbearing; to tyrannize; to bluster; to swell with conscious superiority or haughtiness; -- often with over; as, to domineer over dependents.
quotes: Go to the feast, revel and domineer.
His wishes tend abroad to roam, And hers to domineer at home.
